en.tldr: This paper studies the symmetry properties and critical points of the second Neumann eigenfunction on isosceles trapezoids, parallelograms, and kite domains, providing affirmative evidence for the Hot Spots Conjecture.
en.motivation: To investigate symmetry properties of the second Neumann eigenfunction on specific geometric domains and verify the Hot Spots Conjecture for these domains.
en.method: Combines symmetry decomposition, eigenvalue comparison, and the continuity method to analyze symmetry patterns under varying geometric parameters.
en.result: For isosceles trapezoids, symmetry changes with base angle and height; for parallelograms, eigenfunction is centrally antisymmetric with no non-vertex critical points; for kites, symmetry depends on parameters a and h.
en.help_to_user: Provides a systematic framework and specific criteria for analyzing Neumann eigenfunction symmetry on symmetric geometric domains.

idea_spark.idea: Link geometric parameter variations to abrupt changes in eigenfunction symmetry, establishing phase-transition-like classifications.
idea_spark.risk: Methods heavily rely on domain symmetry; new tools needed for asymmetric domains.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could explore similar symmetry transitions on 3D polyhedra or curved surfaces.

papers.title: Particle Dynamics Driven by Charge Exchange
papers.summary: We introduce and analyse a mathematical model describing the dynamics of particles generated by charge-exchange interactions. The model extends the well-established exchange-driven growth model, previously studied in several works, by allowing for particle densities defined on the entire integer lattice. Despite the many similarities between the two models, substantial differences arise both in their qualitative behaviour and in their mathematical analysis. Under suitable assumptions on the kernel in the collision operator, we establish global well-posedness in the class of nonnegative densities with finite first moment. Moreover, under a detailed balance condition, we investigate the structure of equilibria and analyse their stability by means of entropy methods.

ai.tldr: 本文提出并分析了一个描述电荷交换相互作用产生粒子动力学的数学模型，扩展了已有的交换驱动增长模型，允许粒子密度定义在整个整数格上。
ai.motivation: 扩展交换驱动增长模型，研究在整数格上定义的粒子密度，探索电荷交换相互作用下粒子动力学的数学性质与平衡态行为。
ai.method: 在碰撞算子核的适当假设下，建立具有有限一阶矩的非负密度类中的全局适定性；在细致平衡条件下，利用熵方法研究平衡态结构及其稳定性。
ai.result: 证明了模型的全局适定性，分析了平衡态的结构，并通过熵方法研究了其稳定性，揭示了与原有模型的显著差异。
ai.help_to_user: 为电荷交换驱动的粒子系统提供了严格的数学分析框架，适用于研究类似相互作用系统的动力学与稳定性。

idea_spark.idea: 将交换驱动模型推广到连续格空间，研究不同核函数下的动力学相变，或应用于等离子体或星际介质中的电荷交换模拟。
idea_spark.risk: 模型假设较强，实际物理系统可能偏离细致平衡条件；高维扩展可能带来分析复杂性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将熵方法与其他稳定性工具结合，或探索模型在生物分子交换、社会动力学等跨领域的类比应用。

en.tldr: This paper introduces and analyzes a mathematical model describing particle dynamics generated by charge-exchange interactions, extending the exchange-driven growth model to allow particle densities on the entire integer lattice.
en.motivation: To extend the exchange-driven growth model by considering particle densities defined on the integer lattice, and to investigate the mathematical properties and equilibrium behavior of particle dynamics under charge-exchange interactions.
en.method: Establish global well-posedness in the class of nonnegative densities with finite first moment under suitable assumptions on the collision kernel; investigate equilibrium structure and analyze stability via entropy methods under a detailed balance condition.
en.result: Proved global well-posedness of the model, analyzed the structure of equilibria, and studied their stability using entropy methods, revealing substantial differences from the original model.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous mathematical framework for analyzing charge-exchange-driven particle systems, applicable to studying dynamics and stability in similar interaction systems.

idea_spark.idea: Generalize the exchange-driven model to continuous lattice spaces, study dynamical phase transitions under different kernels, or apply it to simulate charge exchange in plasmas or interstellar media.
idea_spark.risk: Model assumptions are strong; real physical systems may deviate from detailed balance. High-dimensional extensions may introduce analytical complexity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining entropy methods with other stability tools, or exploring analogical applications in cross-disciplinary fields like biomolecular exchange or social dynamics.

papers.title: VecHeart: Holistic Four-Chamber Cardiac Anatomy Modeling via Hybrid VecSets
papers.summary: Accurate cardiac anatomy modeling requires the model to be able to handle intricate interrelations among structures. In this paper, we propose VecHeart, a unified framework for holistic reconstruction and generation of four-chamber cardiac structures. To overcome the limitations of current feed-forward implicit methods, specifically their restriction to single-object modeling and their neglect of inter-part correlations, we introduce Hybrid Part Transformer, which leverages part-specific learnable queries and interleaved attention to capture complex inter-chamber dependencies. Furthermore, we propose Anatomical Completion Masking and Modality Alignment strategies, enabling the model to infer complete four-chamber structures from partial, sparse, or noisy observations, even when certain anatomical parts are entirely missing. VecHeart also seamlessly extends to 3D+t dynamic mesh sequence generation, demonstrating exceptional versatility. Experiments show that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance, maintaining high-fidelity reconstruction across diverse challenging scenarios. Code will be released.

ai.tldr: VecHeart是一个通过混合向量集实现四腔心脏整体解剖建模的统一框架，能处理部分、稀疏或噪声观测下的完整结构推断与动态网格生成。
ai.motivation: 当前前馈隐式方法局限于单对象建模且忽略部件间关联，无法准确处理心脏结构间的复杂相互关系。
ai.method: 引入混合部件Transformer，利用部件特定可学习查询和交错注意力捕捉腔室间依赖；提出解剖补全掩码与模态对齐策略，支持从残缺观测中推断完整结构。
ai.result: 在多样化挑战场景下实现最先进性能，保持高保真重建，并可扩展至3D+时间动态网格序列生成。
ai.help_to_user: 为心脏影像分析提供端到端的四腔建模工具，支持临床不完整数据的解剖结构补全与动态模拟。

idea_spark.idea: 将解剖补全任务转化为基于部件间拓扑约束的掩码生成问题，而非单纯图像补全。
idea_spark.risk: 对罕见解剖变异的泛化能力需验证；动态生成时的时间一致性保障未详细说明。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索与血流动力学模拟耦合，生成生理约束下的心脏运动序列。

zh.tldr: VecHeart是一个通过混合向量集实现四腔心脏整体解剖建模的统一框架，能处理部分、稀疏或噪声观测下的完整结构推断与动态网格生成。

idea_spark.risk: 对罕见解剖变异的泛化能力需验证；动态生成时的时间一致性保障未详细说明。

en.tldr: VecHeart is a unified framework for holistic four-chamber cardiac anatomy modeling via hybrid vector sets, enabling complete structure inference from partial/sparse/noisy observations and dynamic mesh generation.
en.motivation: Current feed-forward implicit methods are limited to single-object modeling and neglect inter-part correlations, failing to handle intricate cardiac structural interrelations.
en.method: Introduces Hybrid Part Transformer with part-specific learnable queries and interleaved attention to capture inter-chamber dependencies; proposes Anatomical Completion Masking and Modality Alignment for inferring complete structures from残缺 observations.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art performance with high-fidelity reconstruction across diverse challenging scenarios, and extends to 3D+t dynamic mesh sequence generation.
en.help_to_user: Provides an end-to-end tool for four-chamber cardiac modeling in medical imaging, supporting anatomical completion from clinical incomplete data and dynamic simulation.

idea_spark.idea: Frame anatomical completion as a mask generation problem constrained by inter-part topology rather than mere image completion.
idea_spark.risk: Generalization to rare anatomical variants requires validation; temporal consistency in dynamic generation is not detailed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could explore coupling with hemodynamic simulation to generate physiologically constrained cardiac motion sequences.

papers.title: Statistical Limit Theorems for Axiom A Diffeomorphisms: Mixing, Central Limit Theorem, and Large Deviations
papers.summary: We establish statistical limit theorems for equilibrium states of Axiom A diffeomorphisms, derived from the spectral gap of the Ruelle transfer operator. This paper contains five main results. The first proves the Volume Lemma with explicit two-sided bounds on the Riemannian volume of dynamical Bowen balls. The second establishes exponential decay of correlations with explicit mixing rates. The third proves the Central Limit Theorem with Berry-Esseen bounds at the optimal rate, with an explicit spectral formula for the asymptotic variance and a characterization of its degeneracy through the Livshits coboundary condition. The fourth establishes the Almost Sure Invariance Principle, providing pathwise Brownian approximation with polynomial error. The fifth proves a large deviations principle with rate function given by the Legendre transform of the pressure. The individual results are due to Sinai, Ruelle, Ratner, Denker-Philipp, Gouezel, Kifer, Melbourne-Nicol, and Young; the contribution is their derivation from a single spectral mechanism with explicit dependence on hyperbolicity data. This constitutes Part V of a six-part series on the thermodynamic formalism for hyperbolic dynamical systems.

ai.tldr: 本文为Axiom A微分同胚的平衡态建立了统计极限定理，包括混合性、中心极限定理和大偏差原理，所有结果均源自Ruelle转移算子的谱间隙。
ai.motivation: 统一推导Axiom A系统已有的统计极限定理，通过单一谱机制明确依赖双曲性数据。
ai.method: 利用Ruelle转移算子的谱间隙，推导出体积引理、相关性指数衰减、中心极限定理、几乎必然不变原理和大偏差原理。
ai.result: 获得了明确的混合速率、中心极限定理的最优Berry-Esseen界、渐近方差的显式谱公式，以及由压力Legendre变换给出的速率函数。
ai.help_to_user: 为双曲动力系统的统计极限定理提供了统一且显式的谱推导框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将统计极限定理的证明统一于单一谱机制，并显式关联双曲性数据。
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖于Axiom A的强双曲结构，可能难以直接推广至非一致双曲系统。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可尝试将谱方法应用于具有弱双曲性或部分双曲性的系统，或探索数值验证显式界。

en.tldr: This paper establishes statistical limit theorems for equilibrium states of Axiom A diffeomorphisms, including mixing, central limit theorem, and large deviations, all derived from the spectral gap of the Ruelle transfer operator.
en.motivation: To unify the derivation of known statistical limit theorems for Axiom A systems via a single spectral mechanism with explicit dependence on hyperbolicity data.
en.method: Utilizing the spectral gap of the Ruelle transfer operator to derive the Volume Lemma, exponential decay of correlations, Central Limit Theorem, Almost Sure Invariance Principle, and large deviations principle.
en.result: Obtained explicit mixing rates, optimal Berry-Esseen bounds for the Central Limit Theorem, an explicit spectral formula for the asymptotic variance, and a rate function given by the Legendre transform of the pressure.
en.help_to_user: Provides a unified and explicit spectral derivation framework for statistical limit theorems in hyperbolic dynamical systems.

idea_spark.idea: Unify proofs of statistical limit theorems under a single spectral mechanism and explicitly link them to hyperbolicity data.
idea_spark.risk: The method relies on the strong hyperbolic structure of Axiom A, which may not directly generalize to non-uniformly hyperbolic systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could attempt to apply spectral methods to systems with weak or partial hyperbolicity, or explore numerical validation of explicit bounds.

keywords_raw: central limit theorem with berry-esseen bounds

papers.title: Diagnosable ColBERT: Debugging Late-Interaction Retrieval Models Using a Learned Latent Space as Reference
papers.summary: Reliable biomedical and clinical retrieval requires more than strong ranking performance: it requires a practical way to find systematic model failures and curate the training evidence needed to correct them. Late-interaction models such as ColBERT provide a first solution thanks to the interpretable token-level interaction scores they expose between document and query tokens. Yet this interpretability is shallow: it explains a particular document--query pairwise score, but does not reveal whether the model has learned a clinical concept in a stable, reusable, and context-sensitive way across diverse expressions. As a result, these scores provide limited support for diagnosing misunderstandings, identifying irreasonably distant biomedical concepts, or deciding what additional data or feedback is needed to address this. In this short position paper, we propose Diagnosable ColBERT, a framework that aligns ColBERT token embeddings to a reference latent space grounded in clinical knowledge and expert-provided conceptual similarity constraints. This alignment turns document encodings into inspectable evidence of what the model appears to understand, enabling more direct error diagnosis and more principled data curation without relying on large batteries of diagnostic queries.

ai.tldr: 提出Diagnosable ColBERT框架，通过将ColBERT的令牌嵌入与基于临床知识的参考潜在空间对齐，增强检索模型的可诊断性。
ai.motivation: 生物医学和临床检索不仅需要强大的排序性能，更需要能够发现系统性模型故障并指导数据修正的实用方法。现有ColBERT模型的令牌级交互分数解释性较浅，无法判断模型是否稳定、可复用且上下文敏感地学习了临床概念。
ai.method: 将ColBERT的令牌嵌入与一个基于临床知识和专家提供的概念相似性约束的参考潜在空间进行对齐。
ai.result: 对齐后的文档编码可作为模型理解内容的可检查证据，支持更直接的错误诊断和更原则性的数据管理，无需依赖大量诊断查询。
ai.help_to_user: 为开发者和研究者提供了一种调试和诊断基于ColBERT的检索模型的方法，尤其适用于对可靠性和可解释性要求高的生物医学和临床领域。

idea_spark.idea: 利用外部知识（如临床概念相似性）构建参考空间，作为模型内部表示的“诊断基准”。
idea_spark.risk: 参考潜在空间的构建质量（如专家约束的完备性和准确性）直接影响诊断的有效性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将模型的可解释性从“事后解释单个预测”提升到“事前评估模型的概念掌握程度”，为主动的模型改进和数据收集提供依据。

en.tldr: Proposes Diagnosable ColBERT, a framework that enhances the diagnosability of retrieval models by aligning ColBERT's token embeddings to a reference latent space grounded in clinical knowledge.
en.motivation: Reliable biomedical and clinical retrieval requires practical methods to find systematic model failures and curate training evidence. The token-level interaction scores in ColBERT offer shallow interpretability but do not reveal whether clinical concepts are learned in a stable, reusable, and context-sensitive manner.
en.method: Aligns ColBERT token embeddings to a reference latent space constructed from clinical knowledge and expert-provided conceptual similarity constraints.
en.result: The alignment turns document encodings into inspectable evidence of the model's understanding, enabling more direct error diagnosis and more principled data curation without relying on extensive diagnostic queries.
en.help_to_user: Provides developers and researchers with a method to debug and diagnose ColBERT-based retrieval models, particularly valuable in biomedical and clinical domains where reliability and interpretability are critical.

idea_spark.idea: Use external knowledge (e.g., clinical concept similarities) to build a reference space that serves as a 'diagnostic benchmark' for the model's internal representations.
idea_spark.risk: The quality of the constructed reference latent space (e.g., completeness and accuracy of expert constraints) directly impacts the effectiveness of diagnosis.
idea_spark.inspiration: Elevates model interpretability from 'post-hoc explanation of a single prediction' to 'pre-assessment of the model's conceptual grasp', providing a basis for proactive model improvement and data collection.

papers.title: Align then Refine: Text-Guided 3D Prostate Lesion Segmentation
papers.summary: Automated 3D segmentation of prostate lesions from biparametric MRI (bp-MRI) is essential for reliable algorithmic analysis, but achieving high precision remains challenging. Volumetric methods must combine multiple modalities while ensuring anatomical consistency, but current models struggle to integrate cross-modal information reliably. While vision-language models (VLMs) are replacing the currently used architectural designs, they still lack the fine-grained, lesion-level semantics required for effective localized guidance. To address these limitations, we propose a new multi-encoder U-Net architecture incorporating three key innovations: (1) an alignment loss that enhances foreground text-image similarity to inject lesion semantics; (2) a heatmap loss that calibrates the similarity map and suppresses spurious background activations; and (3) a final-stage, confidence-gated multi-head cross-attention refiner that performs localized boundary edits in high-confidence regions. A phase-scheduled training regime stabilizes the optimization of these components. Our method consistently outperforms prior approaches, establishing a new state-of-the-art on the PI-CAI dataset through enhanced multi-modal fusion and localized text guidance. Our code is available at https://github.com/NUBagciLab/Prostate-Lesion-Segmentation.

ai.tldr: 提出一种新的多编码器U-Net架构，通过文本引导实现3D前列腺病灶分割，在PI-CAI数据集上达到最先进性能。
ai.motivation: 现有方法在多模态融合和病灶级语义指导方面存在不足，导致前列腺病灶分割精度受限。
ai.method: 采用三阶段创新：对齐损失增强前景文本-图像相似性；热图损失校准相似图并抑制背景激活；置信门控多头交叉注意力细化器进行局部边界编辑。
ai.result: 在PI-CAI数据集上持续超越先前方法，通过增强的多模态融合和局部文本指导建立了新的最先进水平。
ai.help_to_user: 为医学影像分析提供了一种结合文本语义指导的精确分割框架，可提升前列腺病灶诊断的可靠性。

idea_spark.idea: 将文本语义注入分割网络，通过置信门控机制实现局部精细化编辑。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于文本描述的准确性和多模态数据的对齐质量。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将语言模型与医学影像分析更深度结合，实现语义引导的精准定位。

en.tldr: Proposes a novel multi-encoder U-Net architecture for text-guided 3D prostate lesion segmentation, achieving state-of-the-art performance on the PI-CAI dataset.
en.motivation: Current methods struggle with reliable multi-modal fusion and fine-grained lesion-level semantic guidance, limiting prostate lesion segmentation accuracy.
en.method: Three key innovations: alignment loss enhancing foreground text-image similarity; heatmap loss calibrating similarity maps and suppressing background; confidence-gated multi-head cross-attention refiner for localized boundary edits.
en.result: Consistently outperforms prior approaches on the PI-CAI dataset, establishing new state-of-the-art through enhanced multi-modal fusion and localized text guidance.
en.help_to_user: Provides a precise segmentation framework combining textual semantic guidance for medical image analysis, improving reliability in prostate lesion diagnosis.

idea_spark.idea: Inject text semantics into segmentation networks and perform localized refinement through confidence-gated mechanisms.
idea_spark.risk: Depends on accuracy of text descriptions and quality of multi-modal data alignment.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires deeper integration of language models with medical image analysis for semantically-guided precise localization.

papers.title: Bifurcations in Isoperimetric Problems with Nonlocal Interactions
papers.summary: We study isoperimetric problems modeled on the liquid drop model, with nonlocal interactions under a volume constraint. While balls are natural critical points, we show that, for an unbounded sequence of radii, non-spherical solutions bifurcate from the family of balls. These new solutions lie arbitrarily close to balls and can have arbitrarily large volume. Conversely, at radii outside this sequence, no bifurcation occurs, and nearby solutions are trivial, arising only from rigid motions.

ai.tldr: 研究具有非局部相互作用的等周问题，发现当半径取特定无穷序列时，球体解会分岔出非球形解，这些解可任意接近球体且体积可任意大。
ai.motivation: 基于液滴模型，在体积约束下研究非局部相互作用的等周问题，探索球体作为临界点之外的解的结构。
ai.method: 分析等周问题在体积约束下的临界点，研究球体解族的分岔行为，识别分岔发生的半径序列。
ai.result: 证明在半径的无穷序列处，球体解会分岔出非球形解；在该序列之外，附近解仅由刚体运动产生，无分岔。
ai.help_to_user: 为理解非局部相互作用下几何形状的稳定性提供了严格的数学框架，揭示了球体解在特定参数下的结构不稳定性。

idea_spark.idea: 将分岔理论应用于非局部等周问题，系统研究球体解的稳定性边界。
idea_spark.risk: 结论依赖于特定的非局部相互作用模型，推广到更一般的核函数可能需要额外分析。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发对生物膜、自组装材料等系统中形状转变的非局部能量建模研究。

zh.motivation: 基于液滴模型，在体积约束下研究非局部相互作用的等周问题，探索球体作为临界点之外的解的结构。

en.tldr: Studies isoperimetric problems with nonlocal interactions, showing that for an unbounded sequence of radii, spherical solutions bifurcate into non-spherical ones that can be arbitrarily close to balls with arbitrarily large volume.
en.motivation: To investigate isoperimetric problems under volume constraint with nonlocal interactions, extending beyond spherical critical points modeled after the liquid drop model.
en.method: Analyzes critical points of isoperimetric problems under volume constraint, examining bifurcation from the family of spherical solutions and characterizing the radii sequence where bifurcation occurs.
en.result: Proves non-spherical solutions bifurcate from balls at an unbounded sequence of radii; outside this sequence, nearby solutions are trivial and arise only from rigid motions.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous mathematical framework for understanding shape stability under nonlocal interactions, revealing structural instability of spherical solutions at specific parameters.

idea_spark.idea: Apply bifurcation theory to nonlocal isoperimetric problems to systematically study stability boundaries of spherical solutions.
idea_spark.risk: Conclusions depend on the specific nonlocal interaction model; generalization to broader kernel functions may require additional analysis.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires modeling of shape transitions in systems like biomembranes and self-assembling materials with nonlocal energies.

papers.title: Quantifying Spacetime Integration across a Partition with Synergy
papers.summary: In service to the mathematical underpinnings of the Information Integration Theory of Consciousness (IIT), we introduce four measures of integration based on the partial information decomposition framework. We compare our measures to current IIT practice in simple deterministic networks. We find synergy-based measures more suitable for IIT's use-case than current practice. Outside IIT, these measures could also be useful as non-IIT-related measures of complexity within discrete dynamical systems.

ai.tldr: 基于部分信息分解框架，提出了四种信息整合度量，用于量化意识的信息整合理论（IIT）中的时空整合，并在简单确定性网络中验证了其优于现有方法。
ai.motivation: 为意识的信息整合理论（IIT）提供更严谨的数学基础，特别是量化跨分区（如大脑区域）的时空整合程度。
ai.method: 利用部分信息分解框架，构建了四种基于协同（synergy）的整合度量指标，并在简单确定性网络上进行测试与比较。
ai.result: 发现基于协同的度量比当前IIT实践中使用的度量更适用于IIT的目标场景，同时这些度量也可作为离散动力系统中非IIT相关的复杂性度量。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了量化信息整合的新数学工具，可用于意识理论建模或一般动力系统复杂性分析。

idea_spark.idea: 将协同度量应用于其他复杂系统（如生物网络、社会网络）的信息流整合分析。
idea_spark.risk: 在高度非线性或大规模系统中计算可能复杂；度量对网络结构和动力学的敏感性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将信息论工具用于跨学科的系统整合研究，例如脑机接口或人工智能系统的集成度评估。

en.tldr: Introduces four synergy-based integration measures using partial information decomposition to quantify spacetime integration for the Information Integration Theory of Consciousness (IIT), showing superiority over current practice in simple deterministic networks.
en.motivation: To provide a more rigorous mathematical foundation for the Information Integration Theory of Consciousness (IIT), specifically for quantifying the degree of spacetime integration across partitions.
en.method: Constructs four integration measures based on synergy within the partial information decomposition framework, and tests them in simple deterministic networks.
en.result: Finds synergy-based measures more suitable for IIT's objectives than current measures, and notes their potential as general complexity measures for discrete dynamical systems.
en.help_to_user: Offers new mathematical tools for quantifying information integration, applicable to consciousness modeling or general dynamical system complexity analysis.

idea_spark.idea: Apply synergy-based integration measures to analyze information flow integration in other complex systems (e.g., biological networks, social networks).
idea_spark.risk: Computational complexity in highly nonlinear or large-scale systems; sensitivity of measures to network structure and dynamics requires further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires using information-theoretic tools for cross-disciplinary integration studies, such as assessing integration in brain-computer interfaces or AI systems.

papers.title: Growing random planar network with oriented branching and fusion
papers.summary: We consider a growing planar network where a tip grows at constant speed, branches at constant rate and inactivates when it meets a branch already created. We only consider here orthogonal branching occurring always in the same direction. This yields a spatial branching property to the growing network. The connected components of the network then form a branching process of rectangles with double immigration. Using a spine approach for a typical rectangle and coupling arguments, the study is boiled down to a one dimensional stick breaking model with aging. We can then prove long time convergence of empirical measure of the family of rectangles after polynomial rescaling. The limiting distribution and speed of convergence can be explicitly described. The proofs also rely on the description of common ancestor of rectangles in the branching structure with double immigration.

ai.tldr: 研究一个具有定向分支与融合的随机平面生长网络，分支仅在正交方向发生，网络连通分量形成具有双重移民的矩形分支过程。
ai.motivation: 探索具有空间分支特性且分支相遇即停止生长的平面网络的长期统计行为。
ai.method: 使用典型矩形的脊柱方法、耦合论证，将问题简化为具有老化的单维棍棒断裂模型，并分析分支结构中矩形的共同祖先。
ai.result: 证明了矩形族经验测度在多项式重标度后的长时间收敛性，可显式描述极限分布与收敛速度。
ai.help_to_user: 为具有定向分支与融合机制的随机生长网络提供了严格的分析框架与收敛结果。

idea_spark.idea: 将平面网络生长问题映射为矩形分支过程，利用双重移民与老化模型获得极限分布。
idea_spark.risk: 模型假设较强（正交定向、恒定速率），实际系统可能涉及随机方向或速率变化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 该框架可能适用于血管网络、晶体生长或通信网络等具有类似分支-融合机制的系统。

idea_spark.risk: 模型假设较强（正交定向、恒定速率），实际系统可能涉及随机方向或速率变化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 该框架可能适用于血管网络、晶体生长或通信网络等具有类似分支-融合机制的系统。

en.tldr: Studies a growing random planar network with oriented branching and fusion, where branching occurs only orthogonally, and connected components form a branching process of rectangles with double immigration.
en.motivation: To investigate the long-term statistical behavior of planar growing networks with spatial branching properties where growth stops upon branch encounter.
en.method: Uses a spine approach for a typical rectangle and coupling arguments to reduce the problem to a one-dimensional stick-breaking model with aging, and analyzes common ancestors in the branching structure.
en.result: Proves long-time convergence of the empirical measure of the family of rectangles after polynomial rescaling, with explicit description of the limiting distribution and convergence speed.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous analytical framework and convergence results for random growing networks with oriented branching and fusion mechanisms.

idea_spark.idea: Map the planar network growth problem to a branching process of rectangles, utilizing double immigration and aging models to obtain limit distributions.
idea_spark.risk: Model assumptions are strong (orthogonal orientation, constant rates); real systems may involve random directions or variable rates.
idea_spark.inspiration: The framework may be applicable to systems like vascular networks, crystal growth, or communication networks with similar branch-fusion mechanisms.

papers.title: Optimal Online and Offline Algorithms for Contextual MNL with Applications to Assortment and Pricing
papers.summary: Selecting which products to display and at what prices is a central decision in retail and e-commerce operations. In many applications, these two choices must be made jointly under limited display capacity and uncertain customer demand. In this paper, we study the joint assortment and pricing problem under a price-based contextual multinomial logit model, where customer preferences depend on both product features and selling prices. Our analysis begins with the construction of a new confidence region for demand estimation under price-dependent features. Building on this result, we develop a pessimistic offline algorithm and SupCB-type online algorithms for joint assortment and pricing optimization. In the offline setting, we establish a suboptimality guarantee governed by local information around the optimal assortment-price pair, rather than by exact coverage of the optimal action. In the online setting, our SupCB-type algorithm improves the best previously known regret bound to $\widetilde{O}(W\sqrt{dT\log N}/L_0)$, and we also provide a computationally simpler Thompson-sampling alternative. When specialized to the assortment-only or pricing-only setting, our bounds recover the near-minimax-optimal rates established in those respective domains, thereby bridging the gap between the mature study on assortment optimization or dynamic pricing and the limited literature on their joint optimization.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了在价格相关的上下文多项Logit模型下的联合品类与定价问题，提出了离线和在线算法，并改进了已知的遗憾界。
ai.motivation: 零售和电商运营中，在有限的展示容量和不确定的客户需求下，需要联合决定展示哪些产品及其价格。现有文献对联合优化的研究有限。
ai.method: 首先构建了价格相关特征下需求估计的新置信区域。基于此，开发了悲观的离线算法和SupCB型在线算法（包括计算更简单的Thompson采样替代方案）用于联合品类与定价优化。
ai.result: 离线设置中，建立了由最优品类-价格对周围的局部信息（而非最优动作的精确覆盖）控制的次优性保证。在线设置中，SupCB型算法将已知最佳遗憾界改进为$\widetilde{O}(W\sqrt{dT\log N}/L_0)$。当专门用于仅品类或仅定价设置时，我们的界恢复了各自领域中已建立的近乎极小极大最优速率。
ai.help_to_user: 为在不确定需求下联合优化产品展示和定价的零售决策者提供了理论保证的算法工具。

idea_spark.idea: 将联合优化问题分解为需求估计和基于估计的决策两个阶段，并通过局部信息而非全局覆盖来保证性能。
idea_spark.risk: 模型假设（如上下文多项Logit）可能与现实需求有偏差；算法性能依赖于问题参数（如$W, d, L_0$）的估计。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将成熟的仅品类或仅定价优化技术进行融合与扩展，以解决更复杂的联合决策问题；Thompson采样为在线算法提供了计算更简单的替代方案。

en.tldr: This paper studies the joint assortment and pricing problem under a price-based contextual multinomial logit model, proposes offline and online algorithms, and improves the known regret bound.
en.motivation: In retail and e-commerce operations, selecting which products to display and at what prices must be made jointly under limited display capacity and uncertain customer demand. The literature on their joint optimization is limited.
en.method: First constructs a new confidence region for demand estimation under price-dependent features. Building on this, develops a pessimistic offline algorithm and SupCB-type online algorithms (including a computationally simpler Thompson-sampling alternative) for joint assortment and pricing optimization.
en.result: In the offline setting, establishes a suboptimality guarantee governed by local information around the optimal assortment-price pair, rather than by exact coverage of the optimal action. In the online setting, the SupCB-type algorithm improves the best previously known regret bound to $\widetilde{O}(W\sqrt{dT\log N}/L_0)$. When specialized to the assortment-only or pricing-only setting, our bounds recover the near-minimax-optimal rates established in those respective domains.
en.help_to_user: Provides algorithm tools with theoretical guarantees for retail decision-makers needing to jointly optimize product display and pricing under uncertain demand.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose the joint optimization problem into demand estimation and decision-making based on the estimate, and guarantee performance via local information rather than global coverage.
idea_spark.risk: Model assumptions (e.g., contextual multinomial logit) may deviate from real-world demand; algorithm performance relies on estimation of problem parameters (e.g., $W, d, L_0$).
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires the fusion and extension of mature assortment-only or pricing-only optimization techniques to address more complex joint decision problems; Thompson sampling provides a computationally simpler alternative for online algorithms.

papers.title: Error-free Training for MedMNIST Datasets
papers.summary: In this paper, we introduce a new concept called Artificial Special Intelligence by which Machine Learning models for the classification problem can be trained error-free, thus acquiring the capability of not making repeated mistakes. The method is applied to 18 MedMNIST biomedical datasets. Except for three datasets, which suffer from the double-labeling problem, all are trained to perfection.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种称为人工特殊智能的新概念，使机器学习模型在分类问题上实现无错误训练，从而避免重复犯错。该方法在18个MedMNIST生物医学数据集上进行了验证，除三个存在双标签问题的数据集外，其余均实现了完美训练。
ai.motivation: 传统机器学习模型在训练过程中会重复犯错，本研究旨在开发一种能够实现无错误训练的方法，提升模型在生物医学图像分类任务中的可靠性。
ai.method: 提出人工特殊智能概念，并将其应用于18个MedMNIST生物医学数据集的分类任务，重点关注模型避免重复错误的能力。
ai.result: 除三个存在双标签问题的数据集外，其余15个数据集均实现了无错误训练，模型达到了完美分类性能。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种实现机器学习模型无错误训练的新思路，特别适用于对可靠性要求高的生物医学图像分类场景。

idea_spark.idea: 通过人工特殊智能机制，使模型具备错误记忆与避免能力，打破传统误差迭代优化模式。
idea_spark.risk: 双标签问题等数据质量问题可能限制方法效果，且完美训练可能带来过拟合风险。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发从错误避免而非仅误差最小化的角度重构学习目标，为高可靠性AI系统提供新范式。

idea_spark.inspiration: 启发从错误避免而非仅误差最小化的角度重构学习目标，为高可靠性AI系统提供新范式。

en.tldr: This paper introduces a new concept called Artificial Special Intelligence, enabling error-free training of machine learning models for classification problems, thus preventing repeated mistakes. The method is validated on 18 MedMNIST biomedical datasets, achieving perfect training on all except three datasets with double-labeling issues.
en.motivation: Traditional machine learning models make repeated mistakes during training. This research aims to develop a method for error-free training to improve model reliability in biomedical image classification tasks.
en.method: Proposes the Artificial Special Intelligence concept and applies it to classification tasks on 18 MedMNIST biomedical datasets, focusing on the model's ability to avoid repeated errors.
en.result: All except three datasets with double-labeling problems achieved error-free training, with models attaining perfect classification performance on the remaining 15 datasets.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new approach for achieving error-free training in machine learning models, particularly suitable for biomedical image classification scenarios requiring high reliability.

idea_spark.idea: Through the Artificial Special Intelligence mechanism, models gain error memory and avoidance capabilities, breaking the traditional iterative error optimization paradigm.
idea_spark.risk: Data quality issues like double-labeling may limit method effectiveness, and perfect training could lead to overfitting risks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires rethinking learning objectives from an error-avoidance perspective rather than mere error minimization, offering a new paradigm for high-reliability AI systems.

papers.title: A Lagrangian framework for canonical analysis for the Holst model with $β= 0$
papers.summary: We perform a canonical analysis of the Holst model for General Relativity, within the framework laid out in arXiv:2401.07307 and arXiv:2010.07725, distinguishing our approach by setting the Barbero parameter to $β=0$ and leaving the lapse and shift functions unconstrained. The $β= 0$ choice is of particular interest because it is viable across all dimensions, providing a necessary foundation for extending the Loop Quantum Gravity formalism beyond $3+1$ dimensions.   Through field decomposition and the projection of the field equations, we derive a system of 37 equations (10 differential constraints, 21 algebraic constraints, and 6 evolution equations) exactly matching the 37 field components to be determined. Moreover, leaving the gauge unfixed reveals that three equations, which are typically identically satisfied under normal evolution, are actually differential constraints whose triviality depends on specific gauge choices. The resulting framework remains fully consistent with the standard $3+1$ decomposition of the Einstein equations without requiring any constraints on the lapse and shift functions.

ai.tldr: 在Barbero参数β=0且不固定规范条件下，对Holst模型进行正则分析，推导出37个方程，为将圈量子引力推广到更高维度奠定基础。
ai.motivation: β=0的选择在所有维度都有效，是扩展圈量子引力形式到3+1维以外所必需的基础。
ai.method: 采用场分解和场方程投影，在拉格朗日框架内进行正则分析，不约束时移和位移函数。
ai.result: 得到37个方程（10个微分约束、21个代数约束、6个演化方程），与待确定的37个场分量精确匹配。发现三个通常被等同满足的方程实际上是微分约束，其平凡性取决于特定规范选择。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了β=0情况下Holst模型的正则形式，揭示了规范选择对约束结构的影响，为高维推广提供了数学基础。

idea_spark.idea: 研究β参数取其他特殊值（如β=±i）时对约束代数的影响。
idea_spark.risk: 高维推广时可能遇到新的拓扑障碍或代数复杂性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 该方法可能启发其他量子引力方案（如弦论、因果集）的正则化研究。

zh.result: 得到37个方程（10个微分约束、21个代数约束、6个演化方程），与待确定的37个场分量精确匹配。发现三个通常被等同满足的方程实际上是微分约束，其平凡性取决于特定规范选择。

idea_spark.risk: 高维推广时可能遇到新的拓扑障碍或代数复杂性。

en.tldr: Canonical analysis of the Holst model with Barbero parameter β=0 and unfixed gauge, deriving 37 equations that provide foundation for extending Loop Quantum Gravity beyond 3+1 dimensions.
en.motivation: The β=0 choice is viable across all dimensions, providing necessary foundation for extending Loop Quantum Gravity formalism beyond 3+1 dimensions.
en.method: Field decomposition and projection of field equations within Lagrangian framework, leaving lapse and shift functions unconstrained.
en.result: Derived system of 37 equations (10 differential constraints, 21 algebraic constraints, 6 evolution equations) exactly matching 37 field components. Revealed three equations typically identically satisfied are actually differential constraints whose triviality depends on specific gauge choices.
en.help_to_user: Provides canonical formulation of Holst model at β=0, reveals impact of gauge choices on constraint structure, establishes mathematical foundation for higher-dimensional extensions.

idea_spark.idea: Investigate effects of other special β values (e.g., β=±i) on constraint algebra.
idea_spark.risk: Higher-dimensional extensions may encounter new topological obstructions or algebraic complexities.
idea_spark.inspiration: Method may inspire canonical studies of other quantum gravity approaches (e.g., string theory, causal sets).

papers.summary: These are lecture notes from the Austral Winter School on Microlocal Analysis and Non-elliptic Fredholm Theory, held at the Australian National University, Canberra, June 30 -- July 11, 2025.

ai.motivation: 记录并传播2025年澳大利亚冬季学校关于微局部分析与非椭圆Fredholm理论的教学内容。
ai.method: 以讲义笔记形式整理课程内容，涵盖理论框架、核心概念与数学工具。
ai.result: 形成系统的教学材料，为非椭圆Fredholm理论与微局部分析领域提供学习资源。
ai.help_to_user: 为数学物理领域的研究者与学习者提供结构化的理论参考资料。

en.result: Production of systematic teaching materials serving as a learning resource for the fields of non-elliptic Fredholm theory and microlocal analysis.

papers.title: GRAFT: Geometric Refinement and Fitting Transformer for Human Scene Reconstruction
papers.summary: Reconstructing physically plausible 3D human-scene interactions (HSI) from a single image currently presents a trade-off: optimization based methods offer accurate contact but are slow (~20s), while feed-forward approaches are fast yet lack explicit interaction reasoning, producing floating and interpenetration artifacts.   Our key insight is that geometry-based human--scene fitting can be amortized into fast feed-forward inference. We present GRAFT (Geometric Refinement And Fitting Transformer), a learned HSI prior that predicts Interaction Gradients: corrective parameter updates that iteratively refine human meshes by reasoning about their 3D relationship to the surrounding scene.   GRAFT encodes the interaction state into compact body-anchored tokens, each grounded in the scene geometry via Geometric Probes that capture spatial relationships with nearby surfaces.   A lightweight transformer recurrently updates human meshes and re-probes the scene, ensuring the final pose aligns with both learned priors and observed geometry.   GRAFT operates either as an end-to-end reconstructor using image features, or with geometry alone as a transferable plug-and-play HSI prior that improves feed-forward methods without retraining.   Experiments show GRAFT improves interaction quality by up to 113% over state-of-the-art feed-forward methods and matches optimization-based interaction quality at ${\sim}50{\times}$ lower runtime, while generalizing seamlessly to in-the-wild multi-person scenes and being preferred in 64.8% of three-way user study. Project page: https://pradyumnaym.github.io/graft .

ai.tldr: GRAFT是一种几何精炼与拟合Transformer，通过预测交互梯度来快速重建单张图像中物理合理的人-场景3D交互，在保持速度的同时显著提升交互质量。
ai.motivation: 现有单图像人-场景交互重建方法存在速度与质量权衡：优化方法准确但慢，前馈方法快但缺乏显式交互推理，导致漂浮和穿透伪影。
ai.method: 将基于几何的人-场景拟合摊销为快速前馈推理，使用Transformer预测交互梯度（校正参数更新），通过几何探针将身体锚定令牌与场景几何关联，并循环更新人体网格。
ai.result: 相比最先进前馈方法交互质量提升高达113%，达到优化方法交互质量的同时运行时间降低约50倍，在64.8%的三方用户研究中被偏好，并能泛化到野外多人场景。
ai.help_to_user: 提供快速且物理合理的人-场景3D交互重建，可作为端到端重建器或可迁移的即插即用先验改进现有方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将迭代优化过程摊销为前馈网络预测，实现速度与精度的平衡。
idea_spark.risk: 对训练数据中未见的复杂交互场景可能泛化不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 为其他需要结合几何约束与学习先验的3D视觉任务提供了混合架构思路。

zh.tldr: GRAFT是一种几何精炼与拟合Transformer，通过预测交互梯度来快速重建单张图像中物理合理的人-场景3D交互，在保持速度的同时显著提升交互质量。
zh.motivation: 现有单图像人-场景交互重建方法存在速度与质量权衡：优化方法准确但慢，前馈方法快但缺乏显式交互推理，导致漂浮和穿透伪影。
zh.method: 将基于几何的人-场景拟合摊销为快速前馈推理，使用Transformer预测交互梯度（校正参数更新），通过几何探针将身体锚定令牌与场景几何关联，并循环更新人体网格。
zh.result: 相比最先进前馈方法交互质量提升高达113%，达到优化方法交互质量的同时运行时间降低约50倍，在64.8%的三方用户研究中被偏好，并能泛化到野外多人场景。
zh.help_to_user: 提供快速且物理合理的人-场景3D交互重建，可作为端到端重建器或可迁移的即插即用先验改进现有方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将迭代优化过程摊销为前馈网络预测，实现速度与精度的平衡。
idea_spark.risk: 对训练数据中未见的复杂交互场景可能泛化不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 为其他需要结合几何约束与学习先验的3D视觉任务提供了混合架构思路。

en.tldr: GRAFT is a Geometric Refinement and Fitting Transformer that reconstructs physically plausible 3D human-scene interactions from a single image by predicting interaction gradients, significantly improving interaction quality while maintaining speed.
en.motivation: Current single-image human-scene interaction reconstruction faces a speed-quality trade-off: optimization methods are accurate but slow, while feed-forward approaches are fast but lack explicit interaction reasoning, producing floating and interpenetration artifacts.
en.method: Amortizes geometry-based human-scene fitting into fast feed-forward inference. Uses a transformer to predict interaction gradients (corrective parameter updates), grounds body-anchored tokens via geometric probes capturing spatial relationships, and recurrently updates human meshes.
en.result: Improves interaction quality by up to 113% over state-of-the-art feed-forward methods, matches optimization-based interaction quality at ~50× lower runtime, preferred in 64.8% of three-way user study, and generalizes to in-the-wild multi-person scenes.
en.help_to_user: Provides fast and physically plausible 3D human-scene interaction reconstruction, operable as an end-to-end reconstructor or as a transferable plug-and-play prior to improve existing methods.

idea_spark.idea: Amortize iterative optimization into feed-forward network predictions to balance speed and accuracy.
idea_spark.risk: May generalize poorly to complex interaction scenarios not seen in training data.
idea_spark.inspiration: Provides a hybrid architecture approach for other 3D vision tasks needing to combine geometric constraints with learned priors.

papers.title: Rigidity, Fluctuations, and Multifractal Structure of Axiom A Systems: SRB Measures, Livshits Rigidity, and Fluctuation Theorems
papers.summary: This paper develops structural consequences of the thermodynamic formalism for Axiom A diffeomorphisms, with four main results. The Pesin Entropy Formula equates the metric entropy of the SRB measure to the sum of positive Lyapunov exponents, with complete proofs of absolute continuity of conditional measures along unstable manifolds. The Multifractal Formalism computes the Hausdorff dimension of Birkhoff average level sets via the Legendre transform of the pressure, extending earlier work of Barreira, Pesin, and Schmeling. The Livshits Theorem characterizes coboundaries through periodic orbit data with optimal Holder regularity and an explicit norm bound in terms of the contraction rate and the Holder exponent. The Gallavotti-Cohen Fluctuation Theorem establishes the linear symmetry relating the rate function at opposite values of the entropy production rate, with explicit bounds from the spectral gap. This constitutes Part VI, the final installment, of a six-part series on the thermodynamic formalism for hyperbolic dynamical systems.

ai.tldr: 本文作为六部曲的终篇，基于热力学形式体系，为Axiom A微分同胚建立了四个核心结构结果：Pesin熵公式、多重分形形式体系、Livshits刚性定理和Gallavotti-Cohen涨落定理。
ai.motivation: 旨在系统阐述双曲动力系统中热力学形式体系的结构性推论，完成该系列研究的理论闭环。
ai.method: 运用热力学形式体系、谱间隙分析、Legendre变换以及周期轨道数据，对SRB测度、不稳定流形上的条件测度绝对连续性、Birkhoff平均水平集的Hausdorff维数及熵产生率涨落的对称性进行严格推导。
ai.result: 完整证明了Pesin熵公式，扩展了多重分形形式体系，给出了Livshits定理中上同调的最优Hölder正则性与显式范数界，并建立了具有显式谱间隙界的Gallavotti-Cohen涨落定理。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究双曲动力系统的熵、维数、刚性与统计涨落提供了统一的理论框架与严格工具。

idea_spark.idea: 将Livshits定理的周期性数据表征与多重分形维数计算结合，可能揭示更精细的动力学不变集结构。
idea_spark.risk: 理论高度抽象，对具体物理或工程系统的直接建模可能需大量简化与适配。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将刚性、涨落与分形结构的研究范式应用于湍流、气候系统等具有多尺度与混沌特性的现实模型。

en.tldr: As the final part of a six-part series, this paper establishes four core structural results for Axiom A diffeomorphisms based on the thermodynamic formalism: the Pesin Entropy Formula, the Multifractal Formalism, the Livshits Rigidity Theorem, and the Gallavotti-Cohen Fluctuation Theorem.
en.motivation: To systematically articulate the structural consequences of the thermodynamic formalism in hyperbolic dynamical systems, completing the theoretical closure of this series of studies.
en.method: Employing the thermodynamic formalism, spectral gap analysis, Legendre transforms, and periodic orbit data to rigorously derive properties of SRB measures, absolute continuity of conditional measures on unstable manifolds, Hausdorff dimension of Birkhoff average level sets, and symmetry of entropy production rate fluctuations.
en.result: Complete proof of the Pesin Entropy Formula, extension of the Multifractal Formalism, optimal Hölder regularity and explicit norm bounds for cohomologies in the Livshits Theorem, and establishment of the Gallavotti-Cohen Fluctuation Theorem with explicit spectral gap bounds.
en.help_to_user: Provides a unified theoretical framework and rigorous tools for studying entropy, dimension, rigidity, and statistical fluctuations in hyperbolic dynamical systems.

idea_spark.idea: Combining the periodic data characterization of the Livshits Theorem with multifractal dimension calculations may reveal finer structures of dynamical invariant sets.
idea_spark.risk: The theory is highly abstract; direct modeling of specific physical or engineering systems may require significant simplification and adaptation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires applying the research paradigm of rigidity, fluctuations, and fractal structures to realistic models with multi-scale and chaotic characteristics, such as turbulence and climate systems.

papers.title: Beyond Indistinguishability: Measuring Extraction Risk in LLM APIs
papers.summary: Indistinguishability properties such as differential privacy bounds or low empirically measured membership inference are widely treated as proxies to show a model is sufficiently protected against broader memorization risks. However, we show that indistinguishability properties are neither sufficient nor necessary for preventing data extraction in LLM APIs. We formalize a privacy-game separation between extraction and indistinguishability-based privacy, showing that indistinguishability and inextractability are incomparable: upper-bounding distinguishability does not upper-bound extractability. To address this gap, we introduce $(l, b)$-inextractability as a definition that requires at least $2^b$ expected queries for any black-box adversary to induce the LLM API to emit a protected $l$-gram substring. We instantiate this via a worst-case extraction game and derive a rank-based extraction risk upper bound for targeted exact extraction, as well as extensions to cover untargeted and approximate extraction. The resulting estimator captures the extraction risk over multiple attack trials and prefix adaptations. We show that it can provide a tight and efficient estimation for standard greedy extraction and an upper bound on the probabilistic extraction risk given any decoding configuration. We empirically evaluate extractability across different models, clarifying its connection to distinguishability, demonstrating its advantage over existing extraction risk estimators, and providing actionable mitigation guidelines across model training, API access, and decoding configurations in LLM API deployment. Our code is publicly available at: https://github.com/Emory-AIMS/Inextractability.

ai.tldr: 本文证明LLM API中常用的不可区分性隐私属性（如差分隐私）不足以防止数据提取攻击，并提出了(l,b)-不可提取性定义和基于排名的提取风险上界估计方法。
ai.motivation: 当前LLM API隐私保护主要依赖不可区分性属性作为代理指标，但这些属性无法充分衡量实际的数据提取风险，需要更直接的提取风险度量方法。
ai.method: 形式化提取与不可区分性隐私的分离，提出(l,b)-不可提取性定义，通过最坏情况提取博弈推导基于排名的提取风险上界估计器，支持目标精确提取及扩展到非目标和近似提取场景。
ai.result: 理论证明不可区分性与不可提取性不可比较，提出的估计器能紧密高效估计标准贪婪提取风险，并为任意解码配置提供概率提取风险上界，实证评估展示了提取风险与不可区分性的关系及优于现有估计器的优势。
ai.help_to_user: 为LLM API部署提供可操作的风险缓解指南，涵盖模型训练、API访问和解码配置等环节的实际隐私保护建议。

idea_spark.idea: 将提取风险量化为诱导模型输出特定长度子串所需的最小预期查询次数。
idea_spark.risk: 实际部署中攻击者可能采用更复杂的查询策略，超出当前最坏情况博弈的假设范围。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发从攻击者视角构建隐私度量，推动隐私保护从理论性质向实际风险防控转变。

en.tldr: This paper demonstrates that indistinguishability properties commonly used in LLM API privacy are insufficient to prevent data extraction attacks, and introduces (l,b)-inextractability definition with rank-based extraction risk upper bound estimation methods.
en.motivation: Current LLM API privacy protection primarily relies on indistinguishability properties as proxy metrics, but these fail to adequately measure actual data extraction risks, necessitating more direct extraction risk measurement approaches.
en.method: Formalizes separation between extraction and indistinguishability-based privacy, proposes (l,b)-inextractability definition, derives rank-based extraction risk upper bound estimator via worst-case extraction game, extending to untargeted and approximate extraction scenarios.
en.result: Theoretically proves indistinguishability and inextractability are incomparable; the proposed estimator provides tight and efficient estimation for standard greedy extraction and upper bounds probabilistic extraction risk for any decoding configuration; empirical evaluation demonstrates relationship between extractability and distinguishability and superiority over existing estimators.
en.help_to_user: Provides actionable mitigation guidelines for LLM API deployment across model training, API access, and decoding configurations for practical privacy protection.

idea_spark.idea: Quantify extraction risk as minimum expected queries required to induce model output of protected l-gram substrings.
idea_spark.risk: Real-world attackers may employ more sophisticated query strategies beyond current worst-case game assumptions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires constructing privacy metrics from attacker perspective, shifting privacy protection from theoretical properties to practical risk prevention.

papers.title: Generalized PT-symmetric nonlinear Dirac equation: exact solitary waves solutions, stability and conservation laws
papers.summary: We derive an exact solitary wave solution for the $\PTb$-symmetric nonlinear Dirac equation with a scalar-scalar interaction. We consider a power-law nonlinearity of the form $|\barΨ\,Ψ|^{k}\,Ψ$ for positive values of $k$.  The system's energy is conserved despite the presence of a gain-loss term, which is quantified by the parameter $Λ$. We show that the $\PTb$-transition point is defined by the solution's existence condition and is independent of the nonlinearity exponent $k$. Furthermore, momentum is conserved, although neither the canonical momentum nor the charge is a conserved quantity. A notable result is that the stationary solution, obtained from the continuity equations, exhibits nonzero momentum in its rest frame. We also derive a moving soliton solution, where the gain-loss parameter allows the soliton's velocity to be precisely chosen so that the moving soliton possesses zero momentum. Finally, we establish that the presence of a gain-loss mechanism and higher-order nonlinearity restrict the stability domain of the solutions.

ai.tldr: 本文推导了具有标量-标量相互作用的PT对称非线性狄拉克方程的精确孤立波解，研究了其稳定性与守恒律。
ai.motivation: 研究PT对称非线性狄拉克方程在增益-损耗项存在下的精确解与守恒性质，探索非线性指数k对系统行为的影响。
ai.method: 采用幂律非线性形式，通过连续性方程推导静止与运动孤子解，分析能量、动量守恒条件及稳定性区域。
ai.result: 获得了精确孤立波解；证明了PT跃迁点与非线性指数k无关；发现静止解在静止系中具有非零动量；增益-损耗参数可调节运动孤子速度使其动量为零；增益-损耗机制与高阶非线性会限制解的稳定性区域。
ai.help_to_user: 为PT对称非线性狄拉克系统的精确解析求解与稳定性分析提供了系统框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将增益-损耗参数作为孤子速度的调节器，实现零动量运动孤子的精确构造。
idea_spark.risk: 高阶非线性与增益-损耗机制可能显著压缩解的稳定参数空间，限制实际应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 探索PT对称性与非线性相互作用共同调控的孤子动力学，可能为光学或凝聚态系统中的波操控提供新思路。

zh.motivation: 研究PT对称非线性狄拉克方程在增益-损耗项存在下的精确解与守恒性质，探索非线性指数k对系统行为的影响。

en.tldr: This paper derives exact solitary wave solutions for the PT-symmetric nonlinear Dirac equation with scalar-scalar interaction, and investigates their stability and conservation laws.
en.motivation: To study exact solutions and conservation properties of PT-symmetric nonlinear Dirac equations in the presence of gain-loss terms, and explore the influence of the nonlinearity exponent k on system behavior.
en.method: Using a power-law nonlinearity form, deriving stationary and moving soliton solutions via continuity equations, and analyzing energy/momentum conservation conditions and stability regions.
en.result: Obtained exact solitary wave solutions; proved that the PT-transition point is independent of the nonlinearity exponent k; found that stationary solutions exhibit nonzero momentum in their rest frame; the gain-loss parameter can adjust moving soliton velocity to achieve zero momentum; gain-loss mechanisms and higher-order nonlinearity restrict the stability domain of solutions.
en.help_to_user: Provides a systematic framework for exact analytical solution and stability analysis of PT-symmetric nonlinear Dirac systems.

idea_spark.idea: Using the gain-loss parameter as a regulator of soliton velocity to precisely construct moving solitons with zero momentum.
idea_spark.risk: Higher-order nonlinearity and gain-loss mechanisms may significantly compress the stable parameter space of solutions, limiting practical applications.
idea_spark.inspiration: Exploring soliton dynamics jointly regulated by PT symmetry and nonlinear interactions may provide new ideas for wave manipulation in optical or condensed matter systems.

papers.title: EgoMotion: Hierarchical Reasoning and Diffusion for Egocentric Vision-Language Motion Generation
papers.summary: Faithfully modeling human behavior in dynamic environments is a foundational challenge for embodied intelligence. While conditional motion synthesis has achieved significant advances, egocentric motion generation remains largely underexplored due to the inherent complexity of first-person perception. In this work, we investigate Egocentric Vision-Language (Ego-VL) motion generation. This task requires synthesizing 3D human motion conditioned jointly on first-person visual observations and natural language instructions. We identify a critical \textit{reasoning-generation entanglement} challenge: the simultaneous optimization of semantic reasoning and kinematic modeling introduces gradient conflicts. These conflicts systematically degrade the fidelity of multimodal grounding and motion quality. To address this challenge, we propose a hierarchical generative framework \textbf{EgoMotion}. Inspired by the biological decoupling of cognitive reasoning and motor control, EgoMotion operates in two stages. In the Cognitive Reasoning stage, A vision-language model (VLM) projects multimodal inputs into a structured space of discrete motion primitives. This forces the VLM to acquire goal-consistent representations, effectively bridging the semantic gap between high-level perceptual understanding and low-level action execution. In the Motion Generation stage, these learned representations serve as expressive conditioning signals for a diffusion-based motion generator. By performing iterative denoising within a continuous latent space, the generator synthesizes physically plausible and temporally coherent trajectories. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that EgoMotion achieves state-of-the-art performance, and produces motion sequences that are both semantically grounded and kinematically superior to existing approaches.

ai.tldr: EgoMotion是一个用于第一人称视觉语言运动生成的分层生成框架，通过解耦认知推理与运动生成来解决语义推理与运动建模的梯度冲突问题。
ai.motivation: 第一人称运动生成因感知复杂性而研究不足，同时优化语义推理与运动建模会导致梯度冲突，损害多模态对齐与运动质量。
ai.method: 提出两阶段框架：认知推理阶段使用视觉语言模型将多模态输入映射到离散运动原语空间；运动生成阶段使用扩散模型在连续潜空间中进行迭代去噪，生成运动轨迹。
ai.result: EgoMotion实现了最先进的性能，生成的运动序列在语义对齐和运动质量上均优于现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 为具身智能中第一人称行为建模提供了有效的解耦框架，可用于机器人、虚拟现实等需要语义可控运动生成的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将视觉语言模型作为高层推理器，输出结构化中间表示，再驱动底层生成模型，可缓解多任务优化冲突。
idea_spark.risk: 离散原语空间的设计可能限制动作表达的连续性；两阶段训练需保证表征对齐。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受生物认知与运动控制分离机制启发，为复杂生成任务提供模块化设计范式。

zh.method: 提出两阶段框架：认知推理阶段使用视觉语言模型将多模态输入映射到离散运动原语空间；运动生成阶段使用扩散模型在连续潜空间中进行迭代去噪，生成运动轨迹。
zh.result: EgoMotion实现了最先进的性能，生成的运动序列在语义对齐和运动质量上均优于现有方法。
zh.help_to_user: 为具身智能中第一人称行为建模提供了有效的解耦框架，可用于机器人、虚拟现实等需要语义可控运动生成的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将视觉语言模型作为高层推理器，输出结构化中间表示，再驱动底层生成模型，可缓解多任务优化冲突。
idea_spark.risk: 离散原语空间的设计可能限制动作表达的连续性；两阶段训练需保证表征对齐。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受生物认知与运动控制分离机制启发，为复杂生成任务提供模块化设计范式。

en.tldr: EgoMotion is a hierarchical generative framework for egocentric vision-language motion generation that decouples cognitive reasoning from motion synthesis to address gradient conflicts between semantic grounding and kinematic modeling.
en.motivation: Egocentric motion generation remains underexplored due to perceptual complexity, and simultaneous optimization of semantic reasoning and motion modeling introduces gradient conflicts that degrade multimodal grounding and motion quality.
en.method: A two-stage framework: Cognitive Reasoning stage uses a VLM to project multimodal inputs into a structured space of discrete motion primitives; Motion Generation stage employs a diffusion model to perform iterative denoising in a continuous latent space for trajectory synthesis.
en.result: EgoMotion achieves state-of-the-art performance, producing motion sequences that are semantically grounded and kinematically superior to existing approaches.
en.help_to_user: Provides an effective decoupling framework for first-person behavior modeling in embodied AI, applicable to robotics, VR, and other scenarios requiring semantically controllable motion generation.

idea_spark.idea: Using a VLM as a high-level reasoner to produce structured intermediate representations that drive a low-level generative model can mitigate multi-task optimization conflicts.
idea_spark.risk: Discrete primitive space design may limit continuous action expression; two-stage training requires careful representation alignment.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by biological separation of cognitive and motor control, offering a modular design paradigm for complex generation tasks.

papers.title: DeltaSeg: Tiered Attention and Deep Delta Learning for Multi-Class Structural Defect Segmentation
papers.summary: Automated segmentation of structural defects from visual inspection imagery remains challenging due to the diversity of damage types, extreme class imbalance, and the need for precise boundary delineation. This paper presents DeltaSeg, a U-shaped encoder-decoder architecture with a tiered attention strategy that integrates Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) channel attention in the encoder, Coordinate Attention at the bottleneck and decoder, and a novel Deep Delta Attention (DDA) mechanism in the skip connections. The encoder uses depthwise separable convolutions with dilated stages to maintain spatial resolution while expanding the receptive field. Atrous Spatial Pyramid Pooling (ASPP) at the bottleneck captures multi-scale context. The DDA module refines skip connections through a dual-path scheme combining a learned delta operator for nuisance feature suppression with spatial attention gates conditioned on decoder signals. Deep supervision through multi-scale auxiliary heads further strengthens gradient flow and encourages semantically meaningful features at intermediate decoder stages. We evaluate DeltaSeg on two datasets: the S2DS dataset (7 classes) and the Culvert-Sewer Defect Dataset (CSDD, 9 classes). Across both benchmarks, DeltaSeg consistently outperforms 12 competing architectures including U-Net, SA-UNet, UNet3+, SegFormer, Swin-UNet, EGE-UNet, FPN, and Mobile-UNETR, demonstrating strong generalization across damage types, imaging conditions, and structural geometries.

ai.tldr: DeltaSeg是一种用于多类结构缺陷分割的U型编码器-解码器架构，采用分层注意力策略和深度Delta注意力机制，在两个缺陷数据集上优于12种竞争模型。
ai.motivation: 视觉检测图像中的结构缺陷自动分割面临损伤类型多样、类别极度不平衡和需要精确边界划分的挑战。
ai.method: 提出DeltaSeg架构：编码器使用深度可分离卷积和扩张阶段；瓶颈处采用ASPP捕获多尺度上下文；在跳跃连接中引入深度Delta注意力机制，通过双路径方案结合学习的delta算子和空间注意力门；使用分层注意力策略（SE通道注意力、坐标注意力）；通过多尺度辅助头进行深度监督。
ai.result: 在S2DS（7类）和CSDD（9类）两个数据集上评估，DeltaSeg consistently outperforms 12种竞争架构（包括U-Net、SA-UNet、SegFormer等），在不同损伤类型、成像条件和结构几何形状上表现出强泛化能力。
ai.help_to_user: 为结构健康监测中的多类缺陷分割提供了新的注意力机制和架构设计思路，特别是在处理类别不平衡和精确边界划分方面。

idea_spark.idea: 在跳跃连接中引入条件化的delta算子来抑制无关特征，同时结合解码器信号引导的空间注意力。
idea_spark.risk: 架构相对复杂，计算成本可能较高；在更广泛数据集上的泛化性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将不同注意力机制分层集成，并设计专门针对跳跃连接的特征细化模块，可能提升分割精度。

en.tldr: DeltaSeg is a U-shaped encoder-decoder architecture for multi-class structural defect segmentation, employing a tiered attention strategy and Deep Delta Attention mechanism, outperforming 12 competing models on two defect datasets.
en.motivation: Automated segmentation of structural defects from visual inspection imagery is challenging due to diversity of damage types, extreme class imbalance, and the need for precise boundary delineation.
en.method: Proposes DeltaSeg architecture: encoder uses depthwise separable convolutions with dilated stages; ASPP at bottleneck captures multi-scale context; introduces Deep Delta Attention in skip connections via dual-path scheme combining learned delta operator with spatial attention gates conditioned on decoder signals; employs tiered attention strategy (SE channel attention, Coordinate Attention); uses deep supervision through multi-scale auxiliary heads.
en.result: Evaluated on S2DS (7 classes) and CSDD (9 classes) datasets, DeltaSeg consistently outperforms 12 competing architectures including U-Net, SA-UNet, SegFormer, etc., demonstrating strong generalization across damage types, imaging conditions, and structural geometries.
en.help_to_user: Provides novel attention mechanisms and architectural design insights for multi-class defect segmentation in structural health monitoring, particularly for handling class imbalance and precise boundary delineation.

idea_spark.idea: Introduce a conditioned delta operator in skip connections to suppress nuisance features, combined with spatial attention gates guided by decoder signals.
idea_spark.risk: Relatively complex architecture may incur higher computational cost; generalization on broader datasets requires further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Hierarchical integration of different attention mechanisms and designing specialized feature refinement modules for skip connections may improve segmentation accuracy.

papers.title: Environmental Sound Deepfake Detection Using Deep-Learning Framework
papers.summary: In this paper, we propose a deep-learning framework for environmental sound deepfake detection (ESDD) -- the task of identifying whether the sound scene and sound event in an input audio recording is fake or not. To this end, we conducted extensive experiments to explore how individual spectrograms, a wide range of network architectures and pre-trained models, ensemble of spectrograms or network architectures affect the ESDD task performance. The experimental results on the benchmark datasets of EnvSDD and ESDD-Challenge-TestSet indicate that detecting deepfake audio of sound scene and detecting deepfake audio of sound event should be considered as individual tasks. We also indicate that the approach of finetuning a pre-trained model is more effective compared with training a model from scratch for the ESDD task. Eventually, our best model, which was finetuned from the pre-trained WavLM model with the proposed three-stage training strategy, achieve the Accuracy of 0.98, F1 Score of 0.95, AuC of 0.99 on EnvSDD Test subset and the Accuracy of 0.88, F1 Score of 0.77, and AuC of 0.92 on ESDD-Challenge-TestSet dataset.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种用于环境声音深度伪造检测的深度学习框架，通过微调预训练的WavLM模型并采用三阶段训练策略，在基准数据集上取得了高准确率。
ai.motivation: 环境声音深度伪造检测任务需要区分输入音频中的声音场景和声音事件是否为伪造，现有方法在此任务上的性能有待提升。
ai.method: 探索了不同频谱图、网络架构和预训练模型的影响，最终采用微调预训练WavLM模型的三阶段训练策略。
ai.result: 在EnvSDD测试子集上准确率达0.98，F1分数0.95，AUC 0.99；在ESDD-Challenge-TestSet数据集上准确率0.88，F1分数0.77，AUC 0.92。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了环境声音深度伪造检测的有效框架，证明了微调预训练模型优于从头训练，并区分了声音场景和声音事件检测作为独立任务。

idea_spark.idea: 将声音场景与声音事件的深度伪造检测作为独立任务处理，可能提升整体系统鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 模型在跨数据集泛化能力有限（测试集性能差异明显）；未说明对实时或低质量音频的检测效果。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发探索其他预训练音频模型（如HuBERT、AST）在此任务上的表现，或结合频谱图融合技术进一步提升性能。

en.tldr: This paper proposes a deep-learning framework for environmental sound deepfake detection, achieving high accuracy by fine-tuning the pre-trained WavLM model with a three-stage training strategy.
en.motivation: The task of environmental sound deepfake detection requires identifying whether sound scenes and sound events in audio recordings are fake, with room for improvement in existing methods.
en.method: Explored the impact of individual spectrograms, network architectures, and pre-trained models, ultimately fine-tuning the WavLM model with a proposed three-stage training strategy.
en.result: Achieved accuracy of 0.98, F1 score of 0.95, and AUC of 0.99 on the EnvSDD test subset; accuracy of 0.88, F1 score of 0.77, and AUC of 0.92 on the ESDD-Challenge-TestSet.
en.help_to_user: Provides an effective framework for environmental sound deepfake detection, demonstrating that fine-tuning pre-trained models outperforms training from scratch, and treats sound scene and sound event detection as separate tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Treating deepfake detection of sound scenes and sound events as independent tasks may improve overall system robustness.
idea_spark.risk: Limited cross-dataset generalization (notable performance gap between test sets); no mention of performance on real-time or low-quality audio.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires exploration of other pre-trained audio models (e.g., HuBERT, AST) for this task, or combining spectrogram fusion techniques to further enhance performance.

papers.title: Stochastic $Σ$-convergence in Orlicz setting and Applications
papers.summary: This paper aims to extend the concept of stochastic $Σ$-convergence to the framework of Orlicz-Sobolev spaces in order to deals with coupled stochastic and deterministic homogenization problems in this type of spaces. Thus, this concept is a combination of both well-known $Σ$-convergence [\textit{Acta Math. Sinica, English Series} \textbf{30}(9) 1621-1654] and stochastic two-scale convergence in the mean schemes [\textit{Asympt. Anal. (2025)} \textbf{142}, 291-320]. An application to the stochastic-deterministic homogenization (in the context of ergodic $H$-supralgebra) of a class of highly oscillatory minimizations problems involving integral functionals with convex and nonstandard growth integrands is also given, and some concrete homogenization problems following varied structure hypothesis are deduce from this latter.

ai.tldr: 本文将随机Σ-收敛概念推广至Orlicz-Sobolev空间框架，用于处理该空间中的随机与确定性耦合均匀化问题。
ai.motivation: 结合经典Σ-收敛与随机双尺度平均收敛方案，以处理Orlicz-Sobolev空间中更广泛的随机-确定性耦合均匀化问题。
ai.method: 建立Orlicz框架下的随机Σ-收敛理论，并将其应用于具有凸且非标准增长被积函数的振荡极小化问题的均匀化。
ai.result: 给出了随机-确定性均匀化（在遍历H-超代数背景下）的具体应用，并推导了多种结构假设下的均匀化问题。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理Orlicz-Sobolev空间中随机与确定性因素耦合的均匀化问题提供了新的收敛框架和应用实例。

idea_spark.idea: 在非标准增长条件下，研究随机与确定性振荡耦合的变分问题均匀化。
idea_spark.risk: 理论推导依赖于特定的遍历性假设和空间结构，推广至更一般的非凸或非遍历情形可能存在困难。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将随机分析工具与广义函数空间理论结合，以处理更复杂的多物理场耦合均匀化问题。

en.tldr: This paper extends stochastic Σ-convergence to Orlicz-Sobolev spaces to address coupled stochastic and deterministic homogenization problems in such spaces.
en.motivation: To combine classical Σ-convergence with stochastic two-scale convergence in the mean, enabling treatment of broader stochastic-deterministic coupled homogenization problems in Orlicz-Sobolev spaces.
en.method: Develops stochastic Σ-convergence theory in the Orlicz setting and applies it to homogenization of highly oscillatory minimization problems with convex integrands of nonstandard growth.
en.result: Provides an application to stochastic-deterministic homogenization (in the context of ergodic H-supralgebra) and deduces concrete homogenization problems under varied structural hypotheses.
en.help_to_user: Offers a new convergence framework and applications for homogenization problems coupling stochastic and deterministic factors in Orlicz-Sobolev spaces.

idea_spark.idea: Study homogenization of variational problems with coupled stochastic and deterministic oscillations under nonstandard growth conditions.
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical derivations rely on specific ergodicity assumptions and space structures; generalization to non-convex or non-ergodic cases may be challenging.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining stochastic analysis tools with generalized function space theories to address more complex multiphysics coupled homogenization problems.

papers.title: Industrial Surface Defect Detection via Diffusion Generation and Asymmetric Student-Teacher Network
papers.summary: Industrial surface defect detection often suffers from limited defect samples, severe long-tailed distributions, and difficulties in accurately localizing subtle defects under complex backgrounds. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an unsupervised defect detection method that integrates a Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model (DDPM) with an asymmetric teacher-student architecture. First, at the data level, the DDPM is trained solely on normal samples. By introducing constant-variance Gaussian perturbations and Perlin noise-based masks, high-fidelity and physically consistent defect samples along with pixel-level annotations are generated, effectively alleviating the data scarcity problem. Second, at the model level, an asymmetric dual-stream network is constructed. The teacher network provides stable representations of normal features, while the student network reconstructs normal patterns and amplifies discrepancies between normal and anomalous regions. Finally, a joint optimization strategy combining cosine similarity loss and pixel-wise segmentation supervision is adopted to achieve precise localization of subtle defects. Experimental results on the MVTecAD dataset show that the proposed method achieves 98.4\% image-level AUROC and 98.3\% pixel-level AUROC, significantly outperforming existing unsupervised and mainstream deep learning methods. The proposed approach does not require large amounts of real defect samples and enables accurate and robust industrial defect detection and localization.   \keywords{Industrial defect detection \and diffusion models \and data generation \and teacher-student architecture \and pixel-level localization}

ai.tldr: 本文提出一种结合去噪扩散概率模型与非对称师生网络的无监督缺陷检测方法，通过生成高质量缺陷样本和特征重构差异，在MVTecAD数据集上实现了优异的缺陷定位性能。
ai.motivation: 工业表面缺陷检测面临缺陷样本稀缺、长尾分布严重、复杂背景下细微缺陷难以准确定位等挑战。
ai.method: 1. 数据层面：仅使用正常样本训练DDPM，通过恒定方差高斯扰动和Perlin噪声掩码生成高保真缺陷样本及像素级标注。2. 模型层面：构建非对称双流网络，教师网络提供正常特征稳定表示，学生网络重构正常模式并放大异常区域差异。3. 采用余弦相似度损失与像素级分割监督的联合优化策略。
ai.result: 在MVTecAD数据集上达到98.4%图像级AUROC和98.3%像素级AUROC，显著优于现有无监督及主流深度学习方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种无需大量真实缺陷样本即可实现精准工业缺陷检测与定位的解决方案，缓解了数据稀缺问题。

idea_spark.idea: 利用物理一致的噪声模式（如Perlin噪声）引导缺陷生成，增强生成样本的多样性与真实性。
idea_spark.risk: 生成样本的分布可能与真实缺陷存在偏差；复杂工业场景下的背景干扰可能影响定位精度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将生成模型的数据增强能力与判别模型的表征学习相结合，为小样本工业质检提供新范式。

idea_spark.idea: 利用物理一致的噪声模式（如Perlin噪声）引导缺陷生成，增强生成样本的多样性与真实性。
idea_spark.risk: 生成样本的分布可能与真实缺陷存在偏差；复杂工业场景下的背景干扰可能影响定位精度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将生成模型的数据增强能力与判别模型的表征学习相结合，为小样本工业质检提供新范式。

en.tldr: This paper proposes an unsupervised defect detection method integrating Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model with an asymmetric teacher-student network, achieving superior defect localization performance on the MVTecAD dataset through high-quality defect sample generation and feature reconstruction discrepancy.
en.motivation: Industrial surface defect detection suffers from limited defect samples, severe long-tailed distributions, and difficulties in accurately localizing subtle defects under complex backgrounds.
en.method: 1. Data level: Train DDPM solely on normal samples, generating high-fidelity defect samples with pixel-level annotations via constant-variance Gaussian perturbations and Perlin noise-based masks. 2. Model level: Construct an asymmetric dual-stream network where the teacher provides stable normal feature representations, and the student reconstructs normal patterns while amplifying discrepancies in anomalous regions. 3. Adopt a joint optimization strategy combining cosine similarity loss and pixel-wise segmentation supervision.
en.result: Achieves 98.4% image-level AUROC and 98.3% pixel-level AUROC on the MVTecAD dataset, significantly outperforming existing unsupervised and mainstream deep learning methods.
en.help_to_user: Provides a solution for accurate industrial defect detection and localization without requiring large amounts of real defect samples, alleviating data scarcity issues.

idea_spark.idea: Utilize physically consistent noise patterns (e.g., Perlin noise) to guide defect generation, enhancing the diversity and realism of synthetic samples.
idea_spark.risk: Generated samples may deviate from real defect distributions; background interference in complex industrial scenes could affect localization accuracy.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining the data augmentation capability of generative models with the representation learning of discriminative models offers a new paradigm for few-shot industrial quality inspection.

papers.title: Emergence of rigid Polycrystals from atomistic Systems with general Interactions
papers.summary: We investigate the formation of polycrystalline structures in a class of particle systems. The atomistic energy is modeled as a sum of particle energies that favor atoms being locally isometric to a reference lattice. The discrete frame invariant energy allows for particle configurations in which no underlying lattice is assumed a priori.   We prove a discrete-to-continuum limit for configurations with finite surface-energy scaling by means of $Γ$-convergence. The resulting continuum theory is described by piecewise constant fields encoding the local orientation of the configuration. The limiting energy is concentrated on grain boundaries, corresponding to the interfaces between regions where the microscopic configuration has constant orientation. The associated energy density depends on the orientations of the two grains as well as on the normal to the interface.   Due to our assumptions on the rigid interactions, solid-solid phase transitions with interpolating boundary layers are not energetically favorable; the energy density therefore decomposes into twice the energy density for solid-vacuum transitions.

ai.tldr: 研究一类粒子系统中多晶结构的形成，通过Γ收敛证明有限表面能标度下的离散到连续极限，得到以晶界能量集中为特征的连续理论。
ai.motivation: 探索具有一般相互作用的原子系统中刚性多晶体的形成机制，建立微观粒子能量与宏观连续介质理论之间的数学联系。
ai.method: 采用Γ收敛方法，对具有局部等距参考晶格倾向的粒子系统能量进行离散到连续极限分析，允许无先验晶格假设的粒子构型。
ai.result: 连续极限理论由描述局部取向的分段常数场表示，能量集中于晶界，其密度取决于两晶粒取向和界面法向，且固-固相变因刚性相互作用而不利于能量。
ai.help_to_user: 为多晶材料形成的数学建模提供了严格的离散-连续对应框架，适用于具有一般相互作用的原子系统。

idea_spark.idea: 将无先验晶格假设的原子构型与宏观取向场联系起来，通过界面能量集中描述多晶形成。
idea_spark.risk: 刚性相互作用假设可能限制模型对某些实际材料（如具有显著边界层的系统）的适用性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发对复杂晶体缺陷（如位错、孪晶界）的类似数学处理，或探索非刚性相互作用下的相变行为。

en.tldr: Investigates polycrystal formation in particle systems, proving a discrete-to-continuum limit via Γ-convergence for finite surface-energy scaling, yielding a continuum theory with energy concentrated on grain boundaries.
en.motivation: To understand the emergence of rigid polycrystals from atomistic systems with general interactions, establishing a mathematical link between microscopic particle energies and macroscopic continuum theory.
en.method: Uses Γ-convergence to analyze the discrete-to-continuum limit of particle system energies favoring local isometry to a reference lattice, allowing configurations without a priori lattice assumptions.
en.result: The continuum limit is described by piecewise constant orientation fields, with energy concentrated on grain boundaries depending on grain orientations and interface normal; solid-solid phase transitions are energetically unfavorable due to rigid interactions.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous discrete-continuum correspondence framework for modeling polycrystal formation, applicable to atomistic systems with general interactions.

idea_spark.idea: Linking atomic configurations without prior lattice assumptions to macroscopic orientation fields, describing polycrystal formation through interfacial energy concentration.
idea_spark.risk: Rigid interaction assumption may limit applicability to some real materials (e.g., systems with significant boundary layers).
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires similar mathematical treatment of complex crystal defects (e.g., dislocations, twin boundaries) or exploration of phase transition behavior under non-rigid interactions.

papers.title: Exact Quadratic Penalty Function for Symplectic Eigenvalue Problem
papers.summary: The symplectic eigenvalue problem for symmetric positive-definite (spd) matrices plays a crucial role in various scientific fields, including quantum mechanics and control theory. This paper introduces a trace-penalty minimization method, which transforms the symplectic eigenvalue problem into the unconstrained minimization of the trace-penalty function. We prove the equivalence between the penalty problem and the original constrained optimization problem under mild conditions, in the sense that the second-order stationary points of the trace-penalty function correspond to the solutions of the symplectic eigenvalue problem. Moreover, we develop an algorithm to minimize the trace-penalty function efficiently, which follows the scheme of gradient methods, together with the Barzilai-Borwein (BB) adaptive step-size rule and non-monotone line-search technique. Numerical experiments demonstrate that the proposed algorithm outperforms a wide range of existing methods, such as Riemannian gradient-based methods, in terms of computational efficiency and convergence rate for dense, sparse, and sparse-add-low-rank matrices. These numerical results further demonstrate the great potential of our proposed algorithm, especially in solving large-scale symplectic eigenvalue problems.

ai.tldr: 本文提出一种迹惩罚最小化方法，将辛特征值问题转化为无约束优化问题，并证明其等价性。开发了结合BB步长和非单调线搜索的高效梯度算法，数值实验表明其在计算效率和收敛速度上优于现有方法。
ai.motivation: 辛特征值问题在量子力学和控制论等领域至关重要，但现有方法（如黎曼梯度法）在计算大规模问题时效率有限，需要更高效的求解算法。
ai.method: 将辛特征值问题转化为无约束的迹惩罚函数最小化问题，证明其等价性。设计梯度算法，采用Barzilai-Borwein自适应步长规则和非单调线搜索技术进行高效优化。
ai.result: 数值实验证明，该算法在稠密、稀疏及稀疏加低秩矩阵上，计算效率和收敛速度均优于多种现有方法（如黎曼梯度法），特别适用于大规模辛特征值问题。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种高效求解大规模辛特征值问题的新算法框架，适用于需要快速数值解的物理和工程计算场景。

idea_spark.idea: 利用迹惩罚函数的精确性，结合自适应步长和非单调搜索，在保持问题结构的同时加速收敛。
idea_spark.risk: 理论等价性依赖于温和条件，实际中可能对病态矩阵敏感；非单调搜索可能增加单步计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将经典优化技巧（BB步长、非单调搜索）与结构矩阵问题结合，为大规模科学计算问题提供新的算法设计思路。
