en.tldr: This paper advocates fuzzing as a practical way to improve the quality and robustness of deductive verifiers, demonstrating the idea with the prototype tool AValAnCHE integrated with the VerCors verifier.
en.motivation: As deductive verifiers mature, their user base expands from core developers to external users. en.help_to_user: Provides verifier developers with a practical quality improvement method through fuzzing to enhance reliability and user experience without requiring full formal verification.

idea_spark.idea: Fuzz testing the verifier itself rather than the programs being analyzed by the verifier.
idea_spark.risk: Fuzzing may not cover all edge cases and requires adaptation to the verifier's specific input formats.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires new approaches to quality assurance for formal tools, balancing formal verification with practical testing.

papers.title: From Craft to Kernel: A Governance-First Execution Architecture and Semantic ISA for Agentic Computers
papers.summary: The transition of agentic AI from brittle prototypes to production systems is stalled by a pervasive crisis of craft. We suggest that the prevailing orchestration paradigm-delegating the system control loop to large language models and merely patching with heuristic guardrails-is the root cause of this fragility. Instead, we propose Arbiter-K, a Governance-First execution architecture that reconceptualizes the underlying model as a Probabilistic Processing Unit encapsulated by a deterministic, neuro-symbolic kernel. Arbiter-K implements a Semantic Instruction Set Architecture (ISA) to reify probabilistic messages into discrete instructions. This allows the kernel to maintain a Security Context Registry and construct an Instruction Dependency Graph at runtime, enabling active taint propagation based on the data-flow pedigree of each reasoning node. By leveraging this mechanism, Arbiter-K precisely interdicts unsafe trajectories at deterministic sinks (e.g., high-risk tool calls or unauthorized network egress) and enables autonomous execution correction and architectural rollback when security policies are triggered. Evaluations on OpenClaw and NanoBot demonstrate that Arbiter-K enforces security as a microarchitectural property, achieving 76% to 95% unsafe interception for a 92.79% absolute gain over native policies. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/cure-lab/ArbiterOS.

ai.tldr: Arbiter-K 是一种治理优先的执行架构，将 AI 代理重新概念化为由确定性神经符号内核封装的概率处理单元，通过语义指令集架构实现运行时安全策略强制执行。
ai.motivation: 当前将系统控制循环委托给大语言模型并用启发式护栏修补的编排范式，是导致 AI 代理系统脆弱性的根本原因，阻碍了其从脆弱原型向生产系统的过渡。
ai.method: 提出 Arbiter-K 架构，引入语义指令集架构（ISA）将概率消息具体化为离散指令。内核维护安全上下文注册表，并在运行时构建指令依赖图，实现基于数据流谱系的主动污点传播。
ai.result: 在 OpenClaw 和 NanoBot 上的评估表明，Arbiter-K 将安全作为微架构属性来强制执行，对不安全轨迹的拦截率达到 76% 至 95%，相比原生策略有 92.79% 的绝对增益。
ai.help_to_user: 为构建安全、可控的 AI 代理生产系统提供了一个新的系统架构范式，将治理和安全内置于执行层。

idea_spark.idea: 将非确定性的 AI 模型输出“编译”或“具体化”为确定性的、可分析的指令流，从而为传统系统安全技术（如污点分析）的应用创造条件。
idea_spark.risk: 语义 ISA 的设计和实现复杂度高，可能引入性能开销；对安全策略的完备性定义具有挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考如何为新兴的智能体计算设计类似传统 CPU 的“指令集”和“微架构”，从而将软性的“护栏”转化为硬性的、可验证的架构属性。

en.motivation: The prevailing orchestration paradigm—delegating system control to LLMs and patching with heuristic guardrails—is the root cause of fragility, stalling the transition of agentic AI from brittle prototypes to production systems.
en.method: Proposes the Arbiter-K architecture with a Semantic ISA to reify probabilistic messages into discrete instructions. The kernel maintains a Security Context Registry and constructs an Instruction Dependency Graph at runtime for active taint propagation based on data-flow pedigree.
en.result: Evaluations on OpenClaw and NanoBot show Arbiter-K enforces security as a microarchitectural property, achieving 76% to 95% unsafe trajectory interception, a 92.79% absolute gain over native policies.
en.help_to_user: Provides a novel system architecture paradigm for building secure and governable production AI agent systems, embedding governance and security into the execution layer.

idea_spark.idea: To 'compile' or 'reify' non-deterministic AI model outputs into a deterministic, analyzable stream of instructions, enabling the application of traditional system security techniques like taint analysis.
idea_spark.risk: High design and implementation complexity for the Semantic ISA, potentially introducing performance overhead; challenging to define complete security policies.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires thinking about designing 'instruction sets' and 'microarchitectures' for emerging agentic computers, analogous to traditional CPUs, to transform soft 'guardrails' into hard, verifiable architectural properties.

papers.title: "We are currently clean on OPSEC": Why JD Can't Encrypt
papers.summary: We analyse the 2025 Signalgate leak of sensitive US military information by the Trump administration, addressing why confidentiality was violated (messages leaked to the press) in spite of encryption (Signal), to deepen the socio-technical considerations when designing and deploying encryption. First, we use applied pi-calculus to formally model the boutique secure facility setup requested by the US Defence Secretary, to prove that a leak would not be prevented. We then examine how using a secure channel might still not give overall information security, as, in this case, power imbalances between personnel and officials led to the application of cryptography that compromised their operational security. We look at how cryptographic tools may have instilled a false sense of security, and led officials to "overshare". We then apply this analysis to the Trump administration's general desire to burn through political, legal, and now technical process, and demonstrate geopolitical harms that may arise from such ineffective use of cryptography in a brief use case. We conclude that, even with advancements in usability of cryptographic tools, genuine message security is still out of reach of the "average user".

ai.tldr: 分析2025年Signalgate泄密事件，探讨即使使用Signal加密，敏感军事信息仍被泄露的原因，揭示加密工具在真实政治环境中的局限性。
ai.motivation: 加密工具（如Signal）的广泛部署未能防止美国军事机密泄露，需要从社会技术角度理解加密在实际权力结构中的失效机制。
ai.method: 使用应用π演算对国防部长要求的保密设施进行形式化建模，证明泄密不可避免；分析权力不平衡如何导致加密反而损害操作安全。
ai.result: 加密可能营造虚假安全感，导致官员“过度分享”；在政治流程被绕过的情况下，加密无法为“普通用户”提供真正的消息安全。
ai.help_to_user: 为安全系统设计者提供现实案例，说明仅靠技术加密无法解决权力动态和人为因素导致的信息泄露。

idea_spark.idea: 开发“社会技术安全评估框架”，在部署加密前系统性评估组织权力结构、激励制度和人员行为风险。
idea_spark.risk: 过度依赖形式化证明可能忽略非理性人为因素；政治敏感案例的普遍性结论需谨慎推广。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从政治泄密事件反推加密工具设计原则，例如增加“权力不对称警示机制”或“情境感知分享限制”。

en.tldr: Analyzes the 2025 Signalgate leak of US military information, explaining why encryption (Signal) failed to prevent disclosure, highlighting socio-technical limitations of cryptographic tools in real-world political contexts.
en.motivation: The failure of encryption tools (e.g., Signal) to prevent leakage of sensitive military information necessitates a socio-technical examination of how cryptography fails within actual power structures.
en.method: Formally model the secure facility requested by the US Defense Secretary using applied pi-calculus to prove leaks unavoidable; analyze how power imbalances led cryptography to compromise operational security.
en.result: Encryption can create a false sense of security, leading officials to 'overshare'; when political/legal processes are bypassed, cryptography cannot provide genuine message security for the 'average user'.
en.help_to_user: Provides real-world case evidence for security designers that technical encryption alone cannot address information leaks driven by power dynamics and human factors.

idea_spark.idea: Develop a 'socio-technical security assessment framework' to systematically evaluate organizational power structures, incentives, and human behavior risks before deploying encryption.
idea_spark.risk: Over-reliance on formal proofs may neglect irrational human factors; generalizing from politically sensitive cases requires caution.
idea_spark.inspiration: Derive encryption design principles from political leak cases, e.g., 'power-asymmetry warnings' or 'context-aware sharing restrictions'.
